entity.summary: Australia-based hybrid cryptocurrency exchange developed by Sydney blockchain company Chrono.tech and built around centralized trading infrastructure with Plasma-based technology.
entity.notes: Current official TimeX and Chrono.tech materials describe the service as an Australian Plasma-based hybrid exchange. The earlier Ukraine/CEX seed classification is replaced with Australia/hybrid. The reviewed sources support operation by 2019 but do not establish an exact public launch day.

expected.summary: Ukraine-based centralized exchange launched in 2019 and still operating under the TimeX brand.

changes.summary: Australia-based hybrid cryptocurrency exchange developed by Sydney blockchain company Chrono.tech and built around centralized trading infrastructure with Plasma-based technology.
changes.notes: Current official TimeX and Chrono.tech materials describe the service as an Australian Plasma-based hybrid exchange. The earlier Ukraine/CEX seed classification is replaced with Australia/hybrid. The reviewed sources support operation by 2019 but do not establish an exact public launch day.

events.title: TimeX began operating as Chrono.tech's Australian hybrid exchange
events.description: TimeX began operating in 2019 as a Plasma-based hybrid cryptocurrency exchange developed within the Australian Chrono.tech ecosystem.
events.notes: The reviewed official materials support the 2019 year marker and hybrid-exchange identity but do not establish an exact launch day, so January 1 is retained as a year-level marker.

evidence.title: About TimeX
evidence.notes: Official TimeX material describes the service as an Australian hybrid cryptocurrency exchange developed by Chrono.tech.

evidence.title: TimeX product page
evidence.notes: Chrono.tech identifies TimeX as its Plasma-based exchange combining centralized and decentralized technologies.

evidence.title: TimeX official exchange
evidence.notes: The official domain verifies that the TimeX exchange remains accessible and active.
